notable_pick_trades: None — Cardinals hold all 7 original picks across rounds 1-7
roster_context: Massive rebuild underway after a 3-14 season. New HC Mike LaFleur replacing fired Jonathan Gannon. Kyler Murray being released March 11, leaving a massive QB void. Likely to cut Dalvin Tomlinson, Sean Murphy-Bunting, and possibly James Conner to generate ~$25M+ in additional cap space. Roughly $28-30M in cap space post-Murray release, with avenues to create more. Heavily linked to FA QB Malik Willis. Core pieces to build around: Trey McBride (TE, locked through 2029), Marvin Harrison Jr. (WR), Paris Johnson Jr. (LT), Michael Wilson (WR), Budda Baker (S), Will Johnson (CB), Josh Sweat (EDGE).

needs.explanation: Right tackle is arguably the biggest hole on the roster. Jonah Williams suffered back-to-back season-ending injuries and is a UFA. Kelvin Beachum (also UFA) filled in but is not a long-term answer. The only players under contract who could compete are Demontrey Jacobs and Josh Fryar, neither of whom are starter-caliber. Arizona allowed 59 sacks (28th) and averaged 93.1 rushing YPG (31st). The consensus top OT prospect Francis Mauigoa is the most common mock draft pick for Arizona at #3 overall. Paris Johnson Jr. is solid at LT but RT is barren.
needs.depth: Demontrey Jacobs, Josh Fryar (neither viewed as starters)

needs.explanation: Arizona managed just 30 sacks in 2025, tied for third-fewest in the NFL. Josh Sweat is productive but Baron Browning has been a major disappointment and is a cut candidate ($9.4M cap hit). BJ Ojulari is still recovering from a 2024 torn ACL that stunted his development. The staff has openly stated they are seeking a production bump from their pass rushers. Rueben Bain Jr. and David Bailey are top EDGE prospects linked to Arizona at #3. Even if they don't go EDGE at 3, this must be addressed early.
needs.depth: BJ Ojulari (ACL recovery), Zaven Collins (hybrid LB/EDGE), Eku Leota, Jordan Burch
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 if not taking OT at #3, otherwise Round 2-3

needs.explanation: Kyler Murray is being released March 11 after going 38-48-1 in seven years and missing significant time due to injuries. The only QBs under contract are Jacoby Brissett (bridge veteran who produced statistically from Week 6 on but won just one game) and Kedon Slovis. Arizona is heavily linked to free agent Malik Willis, who could cost $25-33M/year. The 2026 draft class is QB-weak — Fernando Mendoza is the only consensus first-rounder and is going #1 to Las Vegas. Ty Simpson (Alabama) had a good combine meeting with Arizona and could be a Day 2 target. This is a franchise-defining need, though it may not be addressed at #3 due to the weak QB class.
needs.depth: Kedon Slovis only; Murray released, no franchise QB on roster
needs.draft_range_target: Free agency (Willis) + possible Round 2 QB (Ty Simpson)

needs.explanation: Dalvin Tomlinson is a near-certain cut — he's 32, carries a $16.2M cap hit, and posted a dismal 43.2 PFF grade (120th of 134 IDL) with just 1 sack and 3 TFL. Bilal Nichols is also likely to be released (10 games in 2 years, $6M savings). Calais Campbell provided a feel-good return at age 39 but is a UFA turning 40. The interior line will need significant reinforcement. The Cardinals invested first-round picks on the DL in back-to-back years but still need depth and a run-stuffing presence.
needs.current_starter: Dalvin Tomlinson (likely cut), Calais Campbell (UFA, age 40)
needs.depth: Bilal Nichols (likely cut), young DL draft picks from recent years

needs.explanation: Jalen Thompson is an unrestricted free agent after starting 15 games and leading the team with 58 solo tackles. While Budda Baker is locked in through 2027 (8x Pro Bowler), the other safety spot needs addressing. Dadrion Taylor-Demerson and rookie Kitan Crawford are in the wings but unproven. Thompson could be re-signed but may seek a payday elsewhere. If Thompson walks, safety becomes a clear need opposite Baker.
needs.current_starter: Budda Baker (SS, locked up), Jalen Thompson (FS, UFA)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-4 if Thompson not re-signed, free agency option

needs.explanation: Marvin Harrison Jr. missed significant time with a season-ending injury in 2025 but remains the WR1. Michael Wilson had a breakout year and is extension-eligible. Beyond those two, the cupboard is bare — Zay Jones and Greg Dortch are UFAs, and the remaining WRs under contract (Baccellia, Brooks, Weaver, Smith-Marsette) combined for just 12 catches and 126 yards in 2025. A reliable WR3 is needed, though this is not a top-2 need given the Harrison/Wilson core.
needs.current_starter: Marvin Harrison Jr. (returning from injury), Michael Wilson
needs.depth: Extremely thin after top two — Zay Jones, Greg Dortch both UFAs

needs.explanation: James Conner (turning 31) suffered a season-ending foot injury after just 3 games in 2025 and is a strong cut candidate ($7.5M savings, $2.2M dead). Trey Benson was also plagued by injuries. If Conner is released, RB becomes a more pressing need. Jeremiyah Love from Notre Dame has been linked to Arizona at #3, though many analysts believe that's a reach. RB could be addressed in the mid-rounds or free agency (Breece Hall has been linked as a LaFleur reunion).
needs.current_starter: James Conner (cut candidate, 31, foot injury) / Trey Benson (injury-prone)

needs.explanation: Will Johnson (2025 2nd-round pick) had a strong rookie year with 10 pass breakups in 12 games and should be CB1 going forward. Max Melton has been inconsistent over two years and Garrett Williams has durability concerns (missed time in both 2024 and 2025). Sean Murphy-Bunting missed all of 2025 with a knee injury and is a near-certain cut ($7.2M savings). Denzel Burke provides depth. The room is young but health and consistency are concerns. Not a top priority but depth is needed.
needs.current_starter: Will Johnson, Max Melton / Garrett Williams (injury concerns)
needs.depth: Denzel Burke, Sean Murphy-Bunting (likely cut), Elijah Jones

needs.explanation: Center Hjalte Froholdt is entering the final year of his contract and was the only 17-game OL starter. LG Evan Brown also in his final year and missed the last 5 games for personal reasons. Isaiah Adams showed improvement at RG after replacing injured Will Hernandez. Jon Gaines II stepped in capably for Brown. The unit is adequate short-term but lacks long-term certainty at guard and center beyond 2026.
needs.current_starter: Hjalte Froholdt (C, final year), Evan Brown (LG, final year), Isaiah Adams (RG)

free_agency_impact: Releasing Kyler Murray (March 11) — creates massive QB need, ~$54.7M dead cap but clears future salary obligations
Jalen Thompson (S) UFA — if not re-signed, opens safety need opposite Budda Baker
Jonah Williams (OT) UFA — already departed effectively; cements RT as critical need
Kelvin Beachum (OT) UFA — backup RT also gone, deepens OT hole
Calais Campbell (IDL) UFA at age 40 — interior DL needs younger replacement
Dalvin Tomlinson expected cut ($9.4M-$14.5M savings) — amplifies IDL need significantly
Sean Murphy-Bunting expected cut ($7.2M savings) — but young CBs have filled the void
Bilal Nichols expected cut ($5.8M savings) — further depletes DL depth
James Conner possible cut ($7.5M savings, turning 31, foot injury) — would create RB need
Strongly linked to signing FA QB Malik Willis ($25-33M/yr) — could reduce but not eliminate QB draft urgency
Greg Dortch and Zay Jones (WR) both UFAs — thins WR depth behind Harrison/Wilson
Trey McBride extended through 2029 ($76M) — TE is NOT a need
Budda Baker under contract through 2027 ($18M/yr) — SS is set
Josh Sweat under contract ($16.4M) and was Pro Bowl alternate — EDGE1 is set but needs a running mate
scheme_fit_notes: New HC Mike LaFleur is expected to call the offense, bringing a Shanahan/McVay-based scheme that prioritizes outside zone running, play-action, and a mobile QB. His brother Matt is the Packers HC, giving Arizona insider intel on Malik Willis. New DC Nick Rallis will run a defense with creative fronts. EDGE prospects who can rush from multiple alignments (stand-up or hand down) have elevated value. The run-heavy offensive philosophy increases the premium on OT and IOL prospects who excel in zone blocking. A mobile, accurate QB fits the scheme profile — Willis's dual-threat ability is a scheme match.
